summary: Clean sound‑board tape of a strong Spring ’93 show highlighted by an incendiary “Ramble On Rose” and killer jams on Corrina and SOTM.
review: The recording is a clean, mostly well‑balanced sound‑board capture that lets the vocals and instruments come through clearly, despite occasional bass‑drum clipping. Musically the concert shines with a tight first set and a second set that delivers standout moments – especially “Ramble On Rose,” the jam out of “Corrina,” and an emotional “Stuck Inside Of Mobile.” Overall the performance is solid and energetic, making this one of the more memorable 1993 dates.

ai_rating.rationale: The majority of reviews rate the show 4‑5 stars and praise both performance and recording; a few 3‑star notes are outweighed by consistent positive comments about key songs and sound quality.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Clear overall mix with crisp vocals and instrument separation; some listeners note a bass‑drum clipping/over‑hot moment and that the tape isn’t ultra‑crisp, but it remains very listenable.

poor_songs: Lazy River / Way To Go / Corrina sandwich
show_quality.setlist_flow: First set is described as tight and energetic, anchored by a superb “Ramble On Rose.” The second set has mixed opinions—some find it clunky around the Eyes/Way‑To‑Go/Lazy River segment, but the jam out of “Corrina” and the soaring SOTM are universally praised.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Consistently lauded for clean, fluid guitar tone and strong vocals; noted as ‘fired up,’ ‘tight,’ and ‘rock‑solid.’
band_member_comments.Phil: Bass is mixed nicely and provides a solid foundation; a few reviewers call it “mixed very nicely.”
band_member_comments.Keys: Vince (Brent Mydland) praised for excellent piano work, vocal contributions, and overall musicality.
band_member_comments.Drums: Both drummers described as tight and flawless; one reviewer calls the drum sound “flawless” and “tight as a drum.”
